content.text:
I have this error:

Test: page.vitest.spec.ts [pages-vitest] > Table [table] page > Page > fetches the qualified table data
File: file:///Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/src/app/footy/table/%5Btable%5D/page.vitest.spec.ts

Error: [vitest] No "Stack" export is defined on the "@mantine/core" mock. Did you forget to return it from "vi.mock"?
If you need to partially mock a module, you can use "importOriginal" helper inside:

  at Module.TablePage (/Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/src/app/footy/table/[table]/page.tsx:114:10)
  at /Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/src/app/footy/table/[table]/page.vitest.spec.ts:99:13


Shouldn't mocks like this be declared centrally, once, rather than in each test?

input.command: cat vitest.pages.config.ts 2>/dev/null; echo "---setup files---"; grep -r "setupFiles" vitest*.config.ts 2>/dev/null
for f in $(grep -rl "vi.mock('@mantine/core'" /Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/src --include="*.ts*"); do echo "=== $f ==="; grep -A 15 "vi.mock('@mantine/core'" "$f" | head -20; done

content.text: That error is because [page.tsx](src/app/footy/table/%5Btable%5D/page.tsx#L1) now imports `Stack` (added in the recent Mantine/Paper layout commit), but the test's local `@mantine/core` mock only stubs `Group`. Quick fix:

result.output: The file /Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy/src/app/footy/table/[table]/page.vitest.spec.ts has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

RUN  v4.1.10 /Volumes/Development/next-www-toastboy


 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
   Start at  06:12:12
   Duration  5.78s (transform 4.35s, setup 27ms, import 5.60s, tests 10ms, environment 0ms)

content.text:
Fixed and passing.

On centralizing the mock: I'd lean toward **not** fully centralizing, but reducing the duplication that caused this. Right now ~25 files each hand-roll a passthrough mock for whatever Mantine components that page happens to import, so every time a page's imports change, the mock silently drifts out of sync (like today) with no compiler check to catch it. The main tradeoff is that several files (e.g. `error.vitest.spec.tsx`'s `Alert`, `CustomAppShell`'s `AppShell.Header`/`Navbar` with `data-testid`s) rely on bespoke rendering, not a generic passthrough — a single shared mock object can't serve those.

A middle ground that avoids both problems: a shared helper (e.g. `src/tests/mocks/mantineCore.ts`) using a `Proxy` that returns a generic `({children}) => children` stub for *any* property accessed, so tests never need to enumerate components at all — then individual files that need special behaviour (testids, specific markup) `vi.mock` with an override on top of it. Want me to build that and migrate a few of these files as a proof of concept?
